title: Airtable linked record expects array of record IDs
symptom: Creating or updating a record fails with 'INVALID_VALUE_FOR_COLUMN' on a linked record field. Passing a string value instead of an array.
applies_when: Writing to a 'Link to another record' field type
Passing a single record ID as a string
not_this_if: Field is a text or number type
Error is about the field name not existing
root_cause: Airtable's linked record fields always expect an array of record IDs, even when linking to a single record. Passing a bare string like 'rec123' instead of ['rec123'] causes INVALID_VALUE_FOR_COLUMN.
fix_steps: Wrap the linked record value in an array: ['recXXX'] instead of 'recXXX'
Ensure you're using record IDs (starting with 'rec'), not display values
Verify the field type in Airtable — it must be 'Link to another record'
agent_instruction: Wrap the linked record value in an array: use ['recXXX'] instead of 'recXXX'. Linked record fields always require an array, even for a single link.
